reasons: cited as prior rare instance where he bought calls, at $150
mentions.text:
$TSM is a screaming buy.

Look at $NVDA rally -> $AVGO rally -> this will flow into TSM.

$1.3T -> $1.8T given all the backlogs from Oracle/NBIS, etc. after reading all earnings.

I almost never buy calls aside from $GOOG back at $150. But IV is decent ~32 and it’s such a great company.

(Emphasis on dip if you’re copy trading, eg. PCI on 11th into recovery for rate cut).

reasons: improving fundamentals with forward earnings and margins across hyperscalers
mentions.text:
@_visionarius @platochi has a valid thesis too. There's not one correct one for sure, but my core reasoning is that there's improving fundamentals (forward earnings, margins, growth), across the board from hyperscalers like $GOOG, $AMZN down to growth stocks from $HOOD, to $RKLB. 

If companies started missing earnings and revising downward like $FISV, (maybe not to that extent lol) then I would be looking to exit the market instead of buying dips.
